_comment: Endpoint inventory governance for the Dang! router vs Wazuh v4.14.3 OpenAPI spec.

excluded._comment: Spec GET endpoints intentionally NOT wired in the router. Each must have a reason.

reasons./agents/uninstall: Write operation — violates read-only-by-default project constraint. Agent uninstall is a destructive action.
reasons./agents/{agent_id}/key: Returns agent registration key — sensitive credential exposure. Not needed for read-focused analyst workflows.
reasons./security/user/authenticate: Auth is handled by Dang!'s own OAuth layer, not by proxying Wazuh token requests to the browser.

extra._comment: Registry endpoints that exist in the router but are NOT in the Wazuh v4.14.3 OpenAPI spec. These are intentional additions, not drift.

reasons./: Root API info endpoint. Returns Wazuh server metadata (version, cluster name). Not in spec but supported at runtime.
reasons./agents/{agent_id}: Single-agent detail fetch by ID. The spec lists /agents with agent filtering, but the direct ID path works at runtime and is more ergonomic for detail views.
reasons./security/users/{user_id}: Individual user retrieval. The spec only defines PUT on this path, but Wazuh accepts GET at runtime. Added as C-4 convenience endpoint.
reasons./security/roles/{role_id}: Individual role retrieval. Same as users — spec has PUT only, runtime supports GET. Added as C-4 convenience endpoint.
reasons./security/policies/{policy_id}: Individual policy retrieval. Same pattern — spec has PUT only, runtime supports GET. Added as C-4 convenience endpoint.
reasons./security/rules/{rule_id}: Individual RBAC rule retrieval. Same pattern — spec has PUT only, runtime supports GET. Added as C-4 convenience endpoint.
